adds.rationale: The Microsoft-Constellation 20-year PPA to restart Three Mile Island (September 20, 2024) is the quarter's clearest nuclear-wave commercial milestone; CEG directly owns and operates the nuclear fleet, complementing URA's uranium-supply exposure with a nuclear-utility position benefiting from AI-data-center baseload demand.

news_evidence.summary: Constellation announces a 20-year, 835 MW PPA with Microsoft to restart Three Mile Island Unit 1 — the first US nuclear restart; CEG surged ~8% pre-market.
TMI Unit 1 to return as Microsoft signs a 20-year 835 MW AI data-center PPA; reactor expected online 2028, 100% of output to Microsoft.
Constellation Q2 2024 8-K: the carbon-free nuclear fleet performed at industry-leading levels during a record-hot summer, demonstrating reliability ahead of the new PPA.
CEG began regular-way Nasdaq trading on February 2, 2022 after the Exelon spinoff — a large-cap utility listed >2.5 years before this rebalance.

removes.rationale: QTUM's top holdings are broad semiconductors (Micron, Intel, ARM, STMicro) that duplicate AI-bucket exposure in MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA; the pure-play quantum thesis is more cleanly expressed by IONQ, which just secured its largest government contract ever ($54.5M AFRL, Sept 25, 2024), making the diluted ETF redundant in an 8-ticker watchlist.

news_evidence.summary: IonQ announces a $54.5M AFRL contract — the largest US quantum contract of 2024 — to deliver quantum hardware over four years; QTUM weights IONQ far down its list behind broad semis.
Defiance QTUM top holdings are Micron, Marvell, Intel, ARM, STMicro — broad semiconductors overlapping the existing AI-bucket positions.
QTUM is an 87-stock basket overlapping broad tech ETFs; pure quantum names like IonQ and D-Wave sit far down, diluting the quantum thesis.

rationale_overall: The quarter's dominant event was the Microsoft-Constellation Three Mile Island PPA (Sept 20, 2024), converting nuclear's quiet-buildup narrative into a concrete 20-year hyperscaler offtake contract. URA covers uranium supply but misses the utility/generation side; CEG owns the nuclear fleet and receives guaranteed offtake, so adding it deepens nuclear (URA + CEG) without duplication. To stay at the 8-cap, QTUM is removed: its top holdings (Micron, Intel, ARM) shadow the AI bucket, while the pure-play quantum exposure the profile seeks is now anchored by IONQ after its $54.5M AFRL win. Net: nuclear upgrades from uranium-only to uranium-plus-utility; quantum tightens to the pure-play.
